What is the Spring Autumn Cicada, and why are the justice factions demanding that Fang Yuan hand it over?

According to his accusers, Fang Yuan killed thousands of people to cultivate it, suggesting it grants immense power or longevity, which is why the factions have united to seize it from him before killing him.

How does Fang Yuan physically appear and emotionally react to being surrounded by enemies, and what does this reveal about his temperament?

Fang Yuan wears torn green robes, has disheveled hair, and is covered in blood from numerous wounds, yet he stands calmly like a sculpture with a quiet gaze and expressionless face. This stoic demeanor despite fatal injuries reveals he possesses an iron will, lacks fear of death, and embodies the unrepentant nature of a 'demon' who accepts his fate without panic.

The poem reflects Fang Yuan's philosophical detachment and nihilistic worldview, suggesting that after living 500 years across two worlds, he views worldly achievements and moral judgments as transient illusions ('nothing left'). This indicates he regrets neither his cultivation path nor his crimes, accepting death as a natural end rather than a punishment, which reinforces his identity as an unrepentant 'demon' who operates beyond conventional morality.

This is manifested in his calm acceptance of death, his refusal to justify his actions to his enemies, and his poetic reflection on life's emptiness rather than seeking forgiveness, confirming that he embodies absolute evil without regret even in his final moments.

What happened to Fang Yuan when he used the Spring Autumn Cicada, and what specifically did he lose in the process?

Fang Yuan successfully traveled back in time 500 years using the Spring Autumn Cicada's power, but the Gu itself dissipated and did not accompany him to the past. According to the text, "Even though he had been reborn, the Spring Autumn Cicada did not come with him," likely because it was a single-use item that required his life, body, and cultivation as the driving force to activate its power.

His character reveals ruthless pragmatism and supreme self-confidence—he accepts the sacrifice because he believes "I have done it in my previous life, why can't I do it in this life," trusting his own capabilities to recreate what he lost rather than fearing the permanent loss.

This causal relationship means the Gu consumes the user's physical existence and likely the artifact itself to send the consciousness back, which explains why Fang Yuan was reborn without the Cicada—it was destroyed in the process of activating its single-use function.

They are distinguished by their durability and usage limits: some Gu "after being used once or even twice or thrice will completely dissipate," while others "can be reused again and again as long as it is not used over its limits," with the Spring Autumn Cicada appearing to be the former type.
